texto: Presumptions of guilt or innocence may sometimes be strengthened or weakened by the place of birth and kind of education and associates a man has grown up with, and good character may at times interpose, and justly save, under suspicion, one who is accused of crime on slight circumstances.
explicacao: Citação de Levi Woodbury sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Presumptions of guilt or innocence may sometimes be strengthened or weakened by the place of birt…” articula educacao, good, character em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como character e man.
